intent: Bring the Arch-only shell tests into CI via a container job, and make test_music_parallelism.sh incapable of silently testing the wrong library after a future directory reorganisation.
scope: linux_configuration/tests/test_music_parallelism.sh: derive the worktree path and assert the stub was sourced
Non-goal: three tests that cannot pass in a fresh container — see risks
changes: test_music_parallelism.sh now derives its temp-worktree path from TARGET_SCRIPT's position relative to REPO_DIR, so the copy automatically mirrors the real directory depth. Previously the depth was hardcoded and a reorganisation broke it silently.

Added two guards to the same test: a pre-flight check that ../../lib/common.sh from the copied script actually resolves to the stub, and a marker file the stub writes when sourced, asserted after every run. Either fires loudly instead of letting the script fall back to the real /usr/local/lib/common.sh and report a misleading behavioural failure.
Four more tests referenced pre-reorganisation paths (missing the periodic_background/ segment) and now resolve: hosts-file-monitor, hosts-guard pacman hooks, makepkg_capped, shutdown-timer-monitor.
New arch-tests job runs five Arch-dependent tests in archlinux:base-devel. git is installed first because base-devel does not ship it and actions/checkout needs it.

verification.evidence: All five PASS with no network, so the job needs no package installs beyond git. Before the path repairs only two of these passed (test_makepkg_wrapper.sh, test_pacman_wrapper_security.sh).

verification.command: bash linux_configuration/tests/<6 ubuntu-set tests> and bats linux_configuration/tests/*.bats
verification.evidence: 0 failures and 30/30 bats — the ubuntu job is unaffected by these edits.

verification.command: drift-guard logic checked against three worktree layouts
verification.evidence: Correct depth reaches the stub; the old shallow depth that caused the original bug fires the guard; a hypothetical three-level layout also fires it.

risks: test_i3blocks_efficiency.sh is excluded: it needs strace plus SYS_PTRACE to count execve calls. This could NOT be validated locally because containers on this machine have no DNS (pacman -Sy fails to resolve mirrors), so strace could not be installed. It may well pass on a GitHub runner, but shipping it unverified risked a red job, so it is left out with a comment.

test_hosts_guard_pacman_integration.sh is excluded: after the path repair it fails on a genuine assertion ('wrapper detects when pacman hooks already manage hosts guard'), which is a real finding needing investigation rather than a CI plumbing problem.

test_security_hardening.sh is excluded permanently: it asserts live machine state such as '/etc/hosts is immutable'. It validates a configured host, not the repository, so it can never pass in a fresh container.

The arch-tests job runs as root in a disposable container, which is what makes the pacman and /etc tests safe. Anything added to that list inherits root — worth checking a new test cannot reach outside the container before adding it.
The offline validation used --network=none; the real job has network, so a test that quietly depends on the network would pass locally and could behave differently in CI.
rollback: git revert this commit: the arch-tests job disappears, the four path repairs revert, and test_music_parallelism.sh returns to hardcoded worktree paths without the stub guards.
Validate after rollback: the four reverted tests fail with 'cannot stat'/'No such file' again, and .github/workflows/shell-tests.yml contains only the shell-tests job.
